reasoning: The AAA corporate bond spread to the 10-year Treasury yield is predominantly a mean-reverting financial time-series. As of the last trading day before February 1, 2026 (Jan 30), the spread was 1.12%. Over the last year, the spread has mostly fluctuated between 0.85% and 1.18%, with a middle range around 1.05% - 1.10%. Because 1.12% is slightly above the central tendency of the past year, mean reversion suggests a slightly higher probability of a decrease than an increase. The FRED tool's statistical analysis also detected a mild short-term downward linear trend, predicting a sub-50% probability of an increase over the next week. Given financial markets largely follow a random walk with slight mean reversion in interest rate spreads, the probability is slightly below 0.50. For the early resolution date (1 week), confidence in the downward drift is a bit stronger against the high 1.12 anchor, so I estimate 38%. For the later date (1 month), volatility increases and the forecast approaches the 50% random-walk baseline, so I estimate 44%.
